method: Blind LLM-as-judge panel. Vendors anonymized as Store A–F (judges did not know which brand was which, including Gorgias). 3 independent judges scored each store's recorded answers on a 1–5 rubric (0 = did not answer / escalated / timed out) across FAQ, Policy/returns, and Product-recommendation, plus an overall. Scores below are the mean of the 3 judges.
rubric: Directness (did it answer?), specificity/accuracy, helpfulness/actionability (links, options, next steps), commerce effectiveness (moves the shopper toward a confident purchase).

scores.note: Specific and complete on every axis; real recommendation with interactive add-to-cart cards.

Re-scored on Glamnetic, a live production store. Strong product rec — interactive Add-to-Cart cards, specific multi-item & cross-category recs (nails->lash->liner), bundle reasoning; docked for not adding to cart and missing a shipping ETA in the buy flow. Support: specific policy answers (14-day returns, customs, discount fixes) but gates order-specific issues behind login; human handover only when explicitly asked.
Precise shipping and a clear (if blunt) no-returns policy, but the recommendation only gestured at collections rather than naming a specific item with a buy path; a separate question also timed out.
Excellent shipping specificity, but it deflects returns to 'share your email' and defers product questions to product pages rather than confidently recommending.
Clear, complete returns policy, but vague boilerplate shipping answer and it escalated the product question to a human lead-capture form (no recommendation).
Good, specific next-day delivery answer, but the product question was escalated to an unavailable human (no recommendation); returns not tested.
Not evaluated — bot backend was down on both test days.

key_findings: Speed and quality do not correlate. The fastest FAQ responder (Meta AI, ~5s) scored lowest on quality (2.0) because it doesn't recommend; the highest-quality vendor (Sierra, 5.0) is also the fastest that actually does product recs.
Only Sierra, Gorgias, Yuma and Siena attempt product recommendations; Meta AI and DigitalGenius escalate to a human (product_rec = 0).

Gorgias (us) on Glamnetic — a live production store, not a sales-tuned demo — lands a strong product rec (4.7, just behind Sierra's 5.0) with interactive Add-to-Cart cards and cross-category discovery, and balanced support (policy 4.0). It does not refuse the job: it answers and recommends. Trade-offs to improve: it's the slowest rec-capable vendor (~20s), won't add to cart for the shopper, lacks an in-chat shipping ETA, and gates order-specific support behind login.
Switching the Gorgias data point from the NouriVida demo to the live Glamnetic store makes the comparison fairer (production catalog + guardrails) and is the more defensible 'us' baseline.
